scope: Unified pi-loop package with intelligent Goal, scheduled Loop, structured Plan, scoring, floating panel, and optional ACE bridge

commands.evidence: 201 Node test cases passed across intelligent Goal lifecycle, acceptance discovery, scoring, evidence verification, objectives, confirmation passes, floating panel, provider-retry and overflow ownership, scheduler, Plan safety, arbitration, ACE context, and package boundaries.
TypeScript no-emit validation passed for the unified extension.
Fresh Pi processes loaded /loop status, /goal status, and /plan status through the single extension entry point.
Dry-run package contents include the intelligent engine, scoring tree, scheduler, Plan runtime, skills, and ACE proof assets while excluding the superseded simplified Goal runtime.
The working-tree diff has no whitespace errors.

provedBehaviors: The former score-guided pi-loop engine and floating panel run under /goal and /pi-goal.
The first score is a baseline; later attempts use verified evidence, hard caps, progress trends, and refined next prompts.
Completion claims enter falsification-oriented confirmation passes instead of ending the Goal immediately.
The stop and off management words never become new Goal objectives.
Scheduled /loop tasks defer while Goal or Plan owns autonomy and never overlap another scheduled run.
Plan mode removes Bash and unknown mutation tools and can convert a saved Plan into the intelligent Goal engine.
ACE context and launch events work when compatible storage or an external adapter listener is available.
One pi-loop package registers Goal, Loop, and Plan through a single extension entry point.
knownLimitations: The external pi-ace-adapter package is not bundled or installed in this checkout; the ACE bridge remains optional and fails closed when no listener exists.
Pi 0.80.2 does not expose willRetry to extension agent_end handlers, so pi-loop retains retry candidates through an internal 15-second terminal fallback that is cancelled when the retry starts.
Scheduled tasks run only while Pi is open and expire after seven days.
Intelligent Goal audit history retains the original project-keyed JSONL persistence model.
